title: Polystichum minimum
intro: Polystichum minimum, also known as the dwarf lady fern, is a small species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum minimum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit various biological activities. Polystichum minimum is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Polystichum minimum is classified as a species of fern within the family Dryopteridaceae, order Polypodiales, and class Polypodiopsida. It is native to the mountainous regions of Japan and Taiwan. The classification of Polystichum minimum is based on its unique characteristics, such as its small size and distinctive leaf shape. This species is often found growing in shaded areas with acidic soil, and it is valued for its compact growth habit and attractive fronds. As a member of the Dryopteridaceae family, Polystichum minimum is closely related to other fern species, such as Dryopteris and Ctenitis.
